goal: Clean up and simplify the repository so the documented workspace matches the active backend and frontend structure.
deliverable: Consolidated setup and roadmap docs under docs/
Updated workspace README and repository structure guide
Removed redundant root and backend artifacts
Aligned env templates, ignore files, and dependency manifests
constraints: Preserve pre-existing dirty worktree changes outside this scope
Avoid backend or frontend feature refactors
Keep removals limited to stale, generated, duplicate, or clearly unused assets
acceptance_criteria: README and structure docs reflect the current workspace layout
Development setup and debugging guidance live in one authoritative doc
TODO docs are clearly separated by language under docs/
Legacy root clutter and generated artifacts are removed
Manifest and env template cleanup is conservative and evidence-backed
product_acceptance_criteria: A contributor can identify the active backend, frontend, docs, scripts, and local-only directories from root docs and manifests alone.
manual_spot_checks: Open README.md and confirm workspace entry points and docs links are correct.
Open docs/DEVELOPMENT_SETUP.md and confirm it covers setup and debugging together.
Open docs/TODO.md and confirm language-specific roadmap files are linked.
completion_language_policy: Do not claim broad project refactoring; claim only repository cleanup, documentation consolidation, and conservative manifest hygiene backed by verification.
delivery_truth_contract: Completion claims must be supported by search results, manifest parsing, diff inspection, and filesystem checks.
non_goals: Refactoring backend or frontend business logic
Normalizing unrelated pre-existing deletions in the worktree
Guaranteeing every historical report in the repo is still current beyond the cleaned scope
inference_notes: The request explicitly authorized removal of redundant and generated repo-local artifacts.
Dependency cleanup was limited to packages shown unused by repository search or replaced by actively used equivalents.
